## Forum Threads

Katalyst p/katalystDivyansh Lohia•

3d ago

### Introducing Katalyst - the Agent that runs pipeline on Salesforce.

Hey everyone,

We just launched Katalyst for sales teams on Salesforce.

Simple idea: reps don't hate selling, they hate the hours they lose every week feeding the CRM. Logging calls, fixing stages and close dates, writing next steps, reconstructing what happened on a deal from three weeks ago. The CRM they "quietly hate."

Most tools just made data entry slightly less painful. It's still the rep doing the work.

Katalyst p/katalystDivyansh Lohia•

7d ago [...] Hi, I'm Div, Founder & CEO of Katalyst.  
  
At Datadog, I watched our best enterprise reps closing multi-million-dollar deals - lose hours every week to work that had nothing to do with selling. Updating Salesforce, prepping for QBRs, rebuilding context that already lived in an email or a call recording. The people best at selling were spending the most time not selling.  
I couldn't unsee it.   
  
So we've spent the last year building Katalyst to fix it: working hands-on with sales leaders and reps across teams like Stripe, Atlassian, Justworks, Datadog.  
  
Katalyst is an AI sales agent for teams on Salesforce. It runs 24/7, reads your calls, emails, and calendar, and turns it into action:   
- Salesforce records created and fields updated on its own

Credit score tools

Ramp runs our banking, cards, and payments, and the reconciliation and receipts just handle themselves. We initially used Mercury but moved our card/payments to Ramp as it just a better user experience and easy to cancel subscriptions. Tip - Turn on the auto categorization and receipt matching rules from day one, before the transactions pile up. The reconciliation feels like magic if you set the rules early.

Salesforce (SFDC)

4.4 (8 reviews)

CRM software

Also considered:

HubSpotHubSpotAttioAttio

Katalyst runs on top of Salesforce, we don't ask anyone to rip out the system their whole company already runs on. Reps connect it and the agent keeps the pipeline clean underneath them. Building with Salesforce instead of against it is the entire bet Pro tip for anyone building with Salesforce - Respect how deeply Salesforce is already wired into an enterprise before you try to change anything - handle the permissions and objects properly, rather than trying to replace it. Work with the system of record, not around it.

Katalyst 

used Linear to build Katalyst

•10 reviews [...] Katalyst 

used Linear to build Katalyst

•10 reviews

Honestly, without Linear we would not be where we are. It runs our engineering, automates the whole flow with GitHub, and lets us ship issues fast. We're a team split across India and the US, and Linear is a big part of how that actually works. Pro tip - Wire up the GitHub automation from day one so issues, branches, and reviews move on their own. If your team is remote or across time zones like ours, that automation is what keeps everyone moving without constant check ins.
